---
title: Mainframe (z/OS Connect / MQ) Adapter — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: mainframe
adapter-class: modernization
mission-class: integration
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-04-16'
aspirational: true
---

# Mainframe (z/OS Connect / MQ) Adapter — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `modernization` · **Mission:** `integration` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/modernization-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The Mainframe Adapter is the **legacy z/OS integration edge** of the UIAO substrate. As a modernization-class integration adapter, it maps COBOL records, MQ messages, and CICS transactions to canonical claims via z/OS Connect or an MQ bridge — the canonical mechanism for bringing mainframe-resident data and operations into the same evidence and drift surfaces the substrate uses for cloud workloads.

Federal agencies that operate mainframe systems for HR, financial, benefits, or correspondence workloads typically have decades of business logic embedded in COBOL and CICS that cannot be lift-and-shifted to cloud-native services on a short timeline. This adapter provides the **structural bridge** so the mainframe-resident records and transactions are visible to the same ATO evidence pipeline, drift engine, and compliance reporting as the rest of the customer's modernization scope.

Three primary functions:

1. **COBOL-record canonicalization.** Maps COBOL record layouts (copybooks) to canonical claim shape, translating EBCDIC encoding, packed-decimal fields, and OCCURS clauses into the substrate's normalized representation. One canonical claim per record per execution.
2. **MQ-message ingestion.** Wraps the MQ bridge so messages flowing through documented queue managers become canonical event claims with provenance back to the source queue, message id, and timestamp.
3. **CICS-transaction observation.** Captures CICS transaction metadata (transaction id, terminal, user, completion code) via z/OS Connect APIs, emitting canonical claims that feed the AC-2 / AU-2 control evidence stream for mainframe-resident activity.

This is the **highest-priority FIMF (Federal IT Modernization Fabric) legacy migration adapter** per canon. Activation requires z/OS Connect or MQ bridge infrastructure to be in place and an `on-prem-self-hosted` runner with network access to the mainframe management plane.

**Implementation:** Reserved slot per ADR-049 / FIMF Tier 5. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `cobol-records`, `mq-messages`, `cics-transactions`

**Reads:** z/OS Connect REST APIs for COBOL-record retrieval, the documented MQ bridge for queue-resident messages, and CICS-transaction metadata. mTLS to the z/OS Connect endpoint and authenticated MQ channels per the canonical least-privilege role.

**Emits:** `mainframe-claim-manifest.json` (per-execution canonical claim records covering COBOL, MQ, and CICS surfaces), plus `EvidenceObject` records that feed the CM-8 / AC-2 / AU-2 control evidence stream.

**Does NOT:** modify mainframe configuration (SAF profiles, RACF rules, SMS classes, etc.), inject MQ messages, initiate CICS transactions, modify COBOL programs or copybooks, or write back to mainframe-resident files. The adapter is observation only; mainframe modernization (data migration, application replatforming) is a separate engagement workstream outside this adapter's surface.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `CM-8`, `AC-2`, `AU-2`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **CM-8** Information System Component Inventory | Primary | Per-record COBOL inventory plus MQ-queue and CICS-transaction inventory produces the canonical mainframe-side component view. |
| **AC-2** Account Management | Supporting | CICS-transaction observation surfaces principal-level activity that supports account-management correlation with cloud-side identity. |
| **AU-2** Event Logging | Primary | MQ message ingestion and CICS transaction observation are the canonical evidence sources for mainframe-resident event logging. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `python-3.12` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `on-prem-self-hosted` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

FIMF Tier 5 adapter. Legacy mainframe integration — maps COBOL records and MQ messages to canonical claims via z/OS Connect or MQ bridge. Highest-priority FIMF adapter for federal legacy-migration use cases. Requires z/OS Connect or MQ bridge infrastructure.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-049 — Reserved-slot allocation framework

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
